title: VERILATOR_ROOT breaks relocated installations
body:
Whether relocating an installation should be supported is up for discussion, but I would like to report this anyway, at least to record the problem for others.

The issue is that bin/verilator (the perl script) uses VERILATOR_ROOT to find verilator_bin, so expects it to be set to the installation prefix, but the generated makefile uses VERILATOR_ROOT to find the run-time lib, so expects it to be set to the pkdata dir (which is <prefix>/share/verilator common Linux hosts), which are different when using an installation instead of a source tree.

Now imagine you installed into a prefix 'foo', then `mv foo bar`

Now you run `./bar/bin/verilator --cc a.sv`
you get this, as it can't find the old pkgdatadir, which is the default for VERILATOR_ROOT when it's not in the environment
```
%Error: Cannot find verilated_std.sv containing built-in std:: definitions: foo/share/verilator/include/verilated_std.sv
%Error: This may be because there's no search path specified with -I<dir>.
        ... Looked in:
             foo/share/verilator/include/verilated_std.sv
             foo/share/verilator/include/verilated_std.sv.v
             foo/include/verilated_std.sv.sv
...
%Error: Exiting due to 2 error(s)
```

Ok, fine, I know where it is:
`env VERILATOR_ROOT=bar ./bar/bin/verilator --cc a.sv`
Now you get this, as bin/verilator fails to find verilator_bin:
```
sh: 1: exec: bar/share/verilator/verilator_bin: not found
%Error: Command Failed ulimit -s unlimited 2>/dev/null; exec bar/share/verilator/verilator_bin --cc a.sv
```

I suspect this (setting VERILATOR_ROOT) was the real cause of the failure described in #3933 (and was due to https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/65b632a7dd8927930c85c8236e73d0fb1d9a31d5/ci/ci-win-test.ps1#L2), but was worked around by installing verilated_std.sv in the required place.

I appreciate if you don't care about having the installation relocatable, and there are various ways to work around this (though none that works with --build as well I think). Just reporting for the record, in case you want to think of this or improve the experience.
code_blocks:
sh: 1: exec: bar/share/verilator/verilator_bin: not found
%Error: Command Failed ulimit -s unlimited 2>/dev/null; exec bar/share/verilator/verilator_bin --cc a.sv
